titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 734

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=978 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.571081+00 | translation moa=979 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 10:59:47.561398+00

p.cl: Omnibus has litteras visuris uel audituris . Patricius filius Patricii Comitis de Dunbar’ Salutem .

Noueritis me ueraciter intellexisse per
cartam regis Eadgari . et per plura alia inditia et recognouisse in curia domini regis Alexandri plenum ius Prioris et monachorum Dunelmensium
apud Coldingham’ deo seruientium de uilla et dominico de Swynefaud’ cum eorum pertinentiis . de quibus eos aliquin iniuste uexauit . Hinc est quod
notum facio vniuersitati uestre me deo et sancto Cuthberto et sancte Ebbe et Priori et monachis Dunelmensibus apud Coldingham deo serui
entibus et in perpetuum ibidem seruituris uillam de Swinefuud et totum dominicum eiusdem uille cum omnibus pertinentiis suis . et totum ius
et omne genus iuris . et clamium si quod habui uel habere potui  in eadem uilla . tam in dominicis quam hominibus seruitiis . et homagiis . boscis
et planis . terris cultis et incultis . et omnibus aliis ad dictam uillam et dictum dominicum pertinentibus  quieta clamasse et abiurasse in perpetu
um pro me et heredibus meis sine aliquo retinemento et per fustum et baculum in curia domini regis  eisdem reddidisse . que antecessores mei iniuste
occupauerunt et detinuerunt . ita quod nec ego nec heredes mei nec aliquis ex parte nostra de cetero ius aut clamium in prenominatis uilla et dominico cum pertinen
tiis habere poterimus .

Dicta autem quieta clamatio facta fuit in curia domini regis Alexandri illustris regis Scottorum apud Rokisburk’ tercio kalend’
Aprilis . Anno regni eiusdem regis . Decimo septimo .

vt autem omnia predicta rata et inconcussa permaneant  presens scriptum sigilli
mei appositione roboraui .

Hiis Testibus . Domino Waltero Olifard’ Iusticiario Laodonie . Domino Willelmo domini regis cancellario . Domino
Iohanne de Maccuswel domini regis Camerario . Domino Roberto de Ros’ . Domino Rannulfo de Bonekil . Domino Bernardo fraser . Domino
Thom(a) filio Ran’ . Domino Thoma de Haya . Dauid Clerico domini Walteri olifard’ . et multis aliis .
To all who shall view or hear these letters, Patrick, son of Patrick earl of Dunbar, sends greeting.

Know me faithfully to have understood and recognised through the charter of King Edgar, and by many other proofs, in the court of the lord king, Alexander, the full right of the prior and monks of Durham serving God at Coldingham in respect of the toun and demesne of Swinewood with its pertinents concerning which I have at some time disturbed them. Hence it is that I make known to you all that I, for myself and my heirs, have quitclaimed and renounced for ever, to God, Saint Cuthbert, Saint Ebbe, and the prior and monks of Durham serving God at Coldingham, and who will in perpetuity serve there, the toun of Swinewood and the entire demesne of the same toun, with all its pertinents, and the whole right and every kind of right and claim, if I had or could have had it in the same toun, as well in demesnes as in men, services and homages, woods and plains, lands cultivated and uncultivated, and all other things belonging to the said toun and the said demesne, without any withholding; and I returned by rod and staff in the king's court what my ancestors unawfully appropriated and withheld, so that neither I nor my heirs nor any on our side could have in the future right or claim in the aforementioned toun and demesne with its pertinents.

The said quitclaim was made in the court of the lord king, Alexander, illustrious king of the Scots, at Roxburgh on the third kalends of April [30 March] in the seventeenth year of reign of the same king.
So that everything aforesaid should remain valid and undisputed, I have authenticated the present writing by the application of my seal.
